topic.what: Holistic intrusion detection covering both network and physical process dimensions of industrial control systems
topic.how: Discusses three key challenges encountered while developing a unified holistic detector: discretization of continuous process data, parameterization of detection models, and gathering representative training data for dynamic environments
topic.why_matters: Current IDS focus on isolated characteristics while attacks target both network and physical dimensions; need holistic approaches that cover all ICS aspects simultaneously

characteristics.what_it_measures: Accuracy of different discretization methods for process mining-based IIDS
characteristics.vs_baseline: quantiles achieves 0.77 accuracy vs jenkspy/kmeans at 0.68
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 shows accuracy varies 0.68-0.77 across discretization methods
characteristics.context: Benchmarking on ICS datasets with posterior knowledge

characteristics.what_it_measures: Precision of process mining discretization methods
Standard deviation of packet inter-arrival times in wired vs wireless ICS
characteristics.vs_baseline: Wired baseline σ=0.37ms vs Wireless− σ up to 1.56ms
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 shows σ increases 4x under wireless disturbance
characteristics.context: Dynamic communication medium impacts timing-based IIDS

characteristics.what_it_measures: GPU memory required for holistic LLM-based ICS monitoring
characteristics.context: SWaT dataset full process monitoring attempt

characteristics.what_it_measures: Risk of timing-based IIDS generating false alerts due to channel disturbance
characteristics.vs_baseline: Wired channel serves as stable baseline
characteristics.evidence: Higher σ in wireless- scenario can trigger alerts indistinguishable from attacks
characteristics.context: Dynamic ICS behavior challenges deterministic timing assumptions

good_for: identifying research directions for holistic IIDS
requires: domain expert knowledge for parameter tuning

novelty: Identifies three core challenges for holistic ICS intrusion detection
Quantifies impact of wireless channel variance on timing-based IIDS
Demonstrates discretization method performance depends on detection metric
Shows LLM-based closed-box approaches face resource and interpretability barriers
open_problems: Creating dynamic detection mechanisms for varying ICS environments
Developing unified models covering all ICS dimensions without multiple parallel detectors
Handling non-deterministic ICS behavior in detection systems
Combining alerts from multiple one-dimensional detectors sensibly
